breadth.note: STABLE-narrow vs the pre-market run: still 5 of 11 sectors Leading on 1m RS (XLV +5.9, XLF +4.7, XLI +2.6, XLU +1.9, XLP +1.1) — the same defensive+financials core, but every leader's RS magnitude COMPRESSED further (XLF +5.1→+4.7, XLV +6.0→+5.9, XLU +2.5→+1.9, XLI +3.0→+2.6). The change that matters: XLK climbed Lagging→Weakening (1m RS −1.5→−1.0, 3m +17.6→+18.3) — the first tentative tech-stabilization tell — while XLRE rolled Weakening→Lagging (−0.6→−1.2). XLC/XLE/XLB remain Lagging. Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed.

regime.note: MORNING = the pre-market risk-off wobble is STABILIZING at the margin, not extending. The tell: VIX EASED from the 17.4 pre-market pop back to 16.78 Tier-A (−0.62; web ~16.96), and XLK's RRG cell climbed Lagging→Weakening while QQQ/VGT ticked green intraday (708.24 / 114.80) — EVEN THOUGH the headline index sits ~−0.77% (SPX ~7,446, web). That index-down / VIX-DOWN combination is the signature of an orderly, dealer-dampened de-risk, NOT a panic. It is NOT an all-clear: XLRE just rolled to Lagging, the defensive leaders' RS compressed, and WTI pushed FURTHER to ~$74 (+5.1%, web) — keeping the inflation/CPI tail live into 7/14. Falsifiers: VIX term structure inverting to backwardation (IVTS >1.0); XLK 1m RS failing back below the pre-market low. Two-sided, but the morning read leans 'shakeout holding' over 'trend-break beginning'.

attention.title: Index down but VIX EASED — the pre-market vol pop is deflating; orderly de-risk, not panic
attention.what: VIX 17.4 (pre-market) → 16.78 Tier-A morning (−0.62; web quote ~16.96), yet the tape is still ~−0.77% (SPX ~7,446, web) with tech leading declines and WTI up further to ~$74 (+5.1%). Term structure carried CONTANGO (IVTS ~0.85; re-pull VIX9D/VIX3M live).
attention.why: This is the morning's key change: falling VIX INTO a red tape is the fingerprint of dealer-dampened, orderly selling — the pre-market 'vol expanding' scare is fading, not accelerating. It confirms the contango read (calm/positive-gamma still governs). The line in the sand is unchanged: only IVTS >1.0 (backwardation) would flip this to genuine risk-off. Until then, treat weakness as hedge-able, not capitulatory.

attention.title: First tech-stabilization tell: XLK climbed Lagging→Weakening while QQQ/VGT ticked green intraday
attention.what: XLK RRG cell improved Lagging→Weakening (1m RS −1.5→−1.0, 3m +17.6→+18.3) and QQQ (707.5→708.24) / VGT (114.0→114.8) ticked up intraday even as the index sat red. Meanwhile XLRE rolled the other way (Weakening→Lagging, −0.6→−1.2). Est. from sector + watchlist bars.
attention.why: The most actionable rotation delta this run: the deepest-lagging leadership group (tech) is showing the first sign of RS stabilization off the pre-market low, WHILE a rate-sensitive (REITs) deteriorates. This is early — XLK is still Weakening, not Leading — but it's the tell to watch for the de-risk resolving up. Confirm on XLK 1m RS crossing >0 + QQQ reclaiming its 50-day; negate on XLK falling back below the −1.5 pre-market low.

attention.title: Defensive/financials leadership is STILL the tape — but every leader's RS keeps compressing
attention.what: Same 5 Leading sectors as pre-market (XLV +5.9, XLF +4.7, XLI +2.6, XLU +1.9, XLP +1.1) but each shrank again (XLF +5.1→+4.7, XLV +6.0→+5.9, XLU +2.5→+1.9, XLI +3.0→+2.6). XLC/XLE/XLB remain Lagging; XLY still Weakening (−0.9). Est. from sector-member bars.
attention.why: Leadership is intact but LOSING conviction two runs in a row — a narrowing, defensively-led tape that's neither broadening nor breaking. The pair trade (XLF/XLV over XLK/XLC) still holds, but the shrinking RS spread argues for taking rather than pressing the trade. If the leaders keep bleeding RS AND XLK stabilizes, the rotation edge collapses — that's the setup to reduce the pair, not add.

attention.title: Momentum still split: 5-day deeply negative, 21-day healing — shakeout intact, not a trend-break
attention.what: 5-day still weak (SPMO −7.4 weakest, VGT −3.95, QQQ −3.82) but the 21-day keeps healing (QQQ +0.56, SPMO +3.86, VGT −0.3 from −1.0) and 63-day holds strong (SPMO +28.5, VGT +28.0, QQQ +20.5). MSFT the outlier: 5d +2.7, 21d still −8.0. Est. from watchlist bars.
attention.why: Unchanged-and-confirmed from pre-market: a sharp 5-day drawdown on a medium-term trend that is quietly turning UP = a shakeout, not (yet) a trend-break. The trim trigger remains the 21-day rolling back negative WITH the 5-day — they're still diverging, so this stays a 'don't add, don't panic-sell' zone. One factor unwind moves SPMO/VGT/QQQ together; SPMO carries the most semis beta.

This is the near-term decision cluster — the oil spike layers headline-CPI risk into 7/14. The far-out 7/29 marquee is secondary this week.

attention.why: The oil move is now the persistent macro risk, not a one-day pop: crude holding/extending above $73 keeps headline-CPI risk elevated into 7/14 and is the cleanest hedge for that tail. But for TREND, the RRG still says avoid — only follow energy up if WTI HOLDS >$73 AND XLE 1m RS crosses positive. Watch crude, not the RRG cell, for the next 1–2 sessions.

In a tape where the leaders are the classic defensives (XLV/XLP/XLU) + XLF, REITs are the odd defensive out (rate-driven). Reduce REIT exposure; re-add on XLRE 1m RS crossing back >0 alongside easing yields. Estimate from sector RS, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Energy (XLE): DON'T chase the oil pop for trend — but it's the cleanest inflation/CPI-tail hedge
recommendations.rationale: WTI extended to ~$74 (+5.1%, web) and energy is the top S&P sector (+~3%), yet XLE's trailing RRG is still the worst cell (1m −4.6, 3m −20.6, Lagging). Trend-entry trigger: WTI HOLDS >$73 AND XLE 1m RS crosses positive — until both, it's a crude/geopolitical spike to fade, not a trend turn. Separately, a long-energy or crude tilt is the cleanest hedge for the oil-driven headline-CPI (7/14) risk that just got MORE live as crude pushed higher. Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Momentum complex (SPMO / VGT / QQQ) — shakeout still holding; don't add, don't panic-sell

The actionable trim trigger is the 21-day rolling back NEGATIVE alongside the 5-day — they're still diverging, so hold. The all-clear is QQQ reclaiming its 50-day + XLK RS turning up (which just started to stabilize). Deep-link the momentum lab for the live crossover. Estimate, not advice.

psychology.read: REGIME: an orderly-but-fragile de-risk that is STABILIZING intraday, not deepening. NAME it: a controlled, dealer-dampened de-risk — VIX pulled BACK from the 17.4 pre-market pop to 16.78 Tier-A (web ~16.96) EVEN THOUGH the index sits ~−0.77% (SPX ~7,446, web) and crude pushed further to ~$74 (+5.1%). Falling vol into a red tape = positive-gamma dampening still governs; the term structure is carried CONTANGO (IVTS ~0.85), consistent with the spot easing. CROWD POSTURE: hedged but NOT panicked — the same defensive/financials leadership (XLV/XLP/XLU + XLF) persists, but every leader's RS keeps COMPRESSING (2 runs running) and the first tech-stabilization tell appeared (XLK Lagging→Weakening, QQQ/VGT green intraday). This is a crowd that de-risked into the pop and is now probing whether the shakeout is over — complacency drained but no capitulation, and the reaction function still punishes tech misses (sell-the-news). FALSIFIERS: (1) VIX term structure inverting to BACKWARDATION (IVTS >1.0) = orderly read breaks into genuine risk-off; (2) XLK 1m RS crossing back >0 + QQQ reclaiming its 50-day = the de-risk is over (bullish falsifier); (3) WTI mean-reverting back under $70 removes the inflation/energy tail and de-fangs the 7/14 CPI headline risk; (4) a soft 10y/30y auction or hawkish minutes re-firing the vol pop that just deflated.

VIX term structure carried CONTANGO (IVTS ~0.85, VIX3M ~19; re-pull VIX9D/VIX3M live) = orderly; falsifier is IVTS >1.0
first tech-stabilization tell: XLK Lagging→Weakening (1m RS −1.5→−1.0, 3m +17.6→+18.3); QQQ/VGT ticked green intraday
breadth STABLE-narrow: same 5 sectors Leading (XLV/XLF/XLI/XLU/XLP) but each RS COMPRESSED again; XLRE rolled Weakening→Lagging
oil extended: WTI ~$74 +5.1% (web morning) on the Iran/geopolitical tension — keeps the headline-CPI tail live into 7/14
momentum split HOLDING: 5-day deeply negative (SPMO −7.4) while 21-day heals (QQQ +0.56, SPMO +3.86, VGT −0.3) — shakeout, not trend-break
10y ~4.55% into TODAY's 10y auction + FOMC minutes (2pm) — the near-term rate fork; a tail could re-fire the deflated vol pop
MSFT $383.24 (5d +2.7, only positive 5-day) vs 21d −8.0 — counter-trend into the 7/29 FOMC-day print
Fear&Greed NOT fetched this run — sentiment band unknown, not assumed
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: CPI (7/14) odds shaded so hot+in-line > cool: the crowd stayed positioned FOR de-risk and crude extended to ~$74, biasing headline upward, so an in-line/hot print confirms the trade while a cool print is the squeeze for beaten-down tech (which is just beginning to stabilize). MSFT miss shaded slightly heavier than the (stale) option-implied for sell-the-news asymmetry, compounded by the 7/29 FOMC collision. OPEX pin held 0.52 because VIX eased (supports positive-gamma pinning) but CPI/PPI/oil still sit three days before expiry. All labeled estimates seeded from STALE options (Jul-6) / unverified consensus — re-seed live.

experimental.title: Vol-spot divergence detector: VIX falling INTO a red tape (dealer-dampening flag) (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag sessions where SPX is down materially (e.g. < −0.5%) WHILE VIX also falls — the fingerprint of positive-gamma / dealer-dampened orderly selling (this run: SPX ~−0.77% yet VIX 17.4→16.78). Track the forward base-rate: does 'index-down/VIX-down' precede stabilization (as the divergence implies) more often than 'index-down/VIX-up'? Overlay the sign of the SPY dealer-gamma flip. Inputs: SPX + ^VIX intraday/daily (have VIX), SPY gamma flip from the gamma lab. No owning tool watches the spot-vol sign divergence today; it drove this morning's 'shakeout holding' read. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: VIX term-structure regime flag: contango→backwardation cross (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track IVTS = VIX/VIX3M (and VIX9D/VIX) daily; flag the cross of IVTS through 1.0 (contango→backwardation) as a regime-change trigger, with day-count of the current regime and a base-rate study of forward SPX returns/realized-vol after each cross. This run's whole read hinges on the curve STAYING in contango (IVTS ~0.85 carried); backwardation is the named falsifier. Inputs: ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M series (add VIX9D/3M to rlData). No owning tool watches the curve cross today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Crude/geopolitical shock → energy RRG divergence monitor (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when a sector is a top-1/2 daily performer while its trailing RRG cell is deeply Lagging (this run: XLE +~3% on WTI +5.1% to ~$74 yet 1m RS -4.6 / 3m -20.6) — a one-day event vs trend divergence — then track whether the daily pop leads the RS (trend turn) or fades (spike). Overlay WTI level + its 5/20-day change on XLE relative-strength; trigger 'trend-follow' only when crude HOLDS a level AND 1m RS crosses >0. Inputs: XLE + sector daily bars (have), CL/WTI series (need to add). Recurs whenever a geopolitical oil move hits a lagging energy tape. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Rate-driven rotation gauge: XLK-vs-XLF RS overlaid on the 10y yield (idea)
experimental.note: Method: plot XLK/XLF (and XLK/XLV) relative-strength ratio against the 10y Treasury yield and its 5/20-day change; regress sector RS-momentum on d(10y) to quantify how rate-sensitive the current leadership rotation is, and flag when the correlation breaks (rotation losing its rate driver = possible tech re-entry — which XLK's Lagging→Weakening turn this run hints at). Inputs: XLK/XLF/XLV daily bars (have), ^TNX or 10y yield series (need to add to rlData). This run's thesis is explicitly rate+oil-driven — no owning tool ties sector RS to yields today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Double-catalyst vol event-study: FOMC-day + mega-cap earnings collision (idea)
experimental.note: Method: event-study base rates for days when an FOMC decision and a top-10 S&P name's earnings land together (Jul 29 = FOMC + MSFT) — measure realized-vs-implied vol, average |move|, and next-day drift vs single-catalyst days. Inputs: historical FOMC dates + mega-cap earnings dates + SPX/QQQ realized vol + straddle-implied. Quantifies the compounding this run flags qualitatively. Not yet built; promote if the pattern recurs across quarters.
